name: iPaaS Wave 1 Foundation v3
outcome_goal: Build foundational iPaaS infrastructure: shared rate limiter, connection pooling, and incoming webhook receiver

jobs.label: ipaas_w1_rate_limiter
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE: Build a shared rate-limit / throttle library for Praxis connectors.

CONTEXT:
- Praxis connectors (httpx + Pydantic clients in artifacts/connectors/) currently each roll their own retry-on-429 logic.
- The rate_limit_prober at Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/rate_limit_prober.py probes providers but doesn't enforce limits at the call site.
- Auto-retry at Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/auto_retry.py classifies 429/rate-limit as TRANSIENT but doesn't throttle.
- We need a shared library that connectors import instead of implementing their own.

DELIVERABLES:

1. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integrations/rate_limiter.py:
   - TokenBucketLimiter class: configurable tokens_per_second, burst_size per provider_slug
   - async acquire(provider_slug) -> awaits until a token is available
   - sync acquire_sync(provider_slug) -> blocks until token available (for sync connectors)
   - parse_retry_after(headers: dict) -> Optional[float] — parses Retry-After header (seconds or HTTP-date)
   - RateLimitConfig dataclass: provider_slug, tokens_per_second, burst_size, retry_after_buffer_seconds
   - ProviderThrottleRegistry: singleton that holds per-provider TokenBucketLimiter instances
   - get_throttle(provider_slug) -> TokenBucketLimiter (creates with defaults if not registered)
   - record_429(provider_slug, retry_after: Optional[float]) -> drains bucket, schedules refill after retry_after

2. Create Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/092_rate_limit_config.sql:
   - Table: rate_limit_configs (provider_slug TEXT PRIMARY KEY, tokens_per_second REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 10.0, burst_size INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 20, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now())
   - Seed rows for: openai (10 tps, burst 20), anthropic (5 tps, burst 10), google (15 tps, burst 30)

3. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/test_rate_limiter.py:
   - Test token bucket refill rate
   - Test burst capacity
   - Test 429 drain + retry_after
   - Test Retry-After header parsing (seconds and HTTP-date formats)
   - Test concurrent acquire doesn't exceed rate

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
- Use the same module style as Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/auto_retry.py (dataclasses, clear classify/decide pattern)
- Migration follows the pattern in Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/ (plain SQL, numbered)
- Tests use: PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' python3 -m pytest --noconftest -q <test_file>

DO NOT:
- Add async frameworks beyond what's already in the codebase (stdlib asyncio is fine)
- Create abstract base classes or over-engineer the hierarchy
- Touch any existing files — this is all new code

jobs.label: ipaas_w1_connection_pool
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE: Build a shared httpx connection pool and response cache for Praxis connectors.

CONTEXT:
- Connectors in artifacts/connectors/ each create standalone httpx.AsyncClient instances per request.
- The webhook executor at Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integrations/webhook.py uses urllib.request (no pooling).
- The api_task adapter at Code&DBs/Workflow/adapters/api_task.py also uses urllib.request.
- We need a shared pool that connectors and integrations use, plus optional response caching for idempotent GETs.

DELIVERABLES:

1. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integrations/http_pool.py:
   - SharedHTTPPool class (singleton):
     - get_client(base_url: str, timeout: float = 30.0) -> httpx.AsyncClient with connection pooling
     - get_sync_client(base_url: str, timeout: float = 30.0) -> httpx.Client with connection pooling
     - Pool limits: max_connections=100, max_keepalive_connections=20, keepalive_expiry=30
     - close_all() -> closes all pooled clients
   - ResponseCache class:
     - get(cache_key: str) -> Optional[CachedResponse]
     - put(cache_key: str, response: CachedResponse, ttl_seconds: int = 300)
     - CachedResponse dataclass: status_code, headers, body, cached_at
     - cache_key_for(method: str, url: str, params: dict) -> str (only caches GET/HEAD)
     - In-memory LRU with max_size=1000, respects Cache-Control: no-cache
     - evict_expired() -> removes stale entries
   - Module-level convenience:
     - get_pool() -> SharedHTTPPool (lazy singleton)
     - cached_get(url, headers, params, ttl) -> response (pool + cache combined)

2. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/test_http_pool.py:
   - Test client reuse for same base_url
   - Test cache hit/miss
   - Test TTL expiration
   - Test cache bypass for POST/PUT/DELETE
   - Test Cache-Control: no-cache header respected
   - Test pool limits enforced

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
- httpx is already in requirements (used by connector builds)
- Use dataclasses, not Pydantic, for internal types
- Follow the singleton pattern from Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/rate_limit_prober.py
- Tests: PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' python3 -m pytest --noconftest -q <test_file>

DO NOT:
- Replace urllib usage in existing webhook.py or api_task.py (those will be migrated later)
- Add Redis or external cache dependencies — in-memory only
- Create more than 2 files (implementation + tests)

jobs.label: ipaas_w1_webhook_receiver
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE: Build an incoming webhook receiver — an HTTP endpoint that accepts webhooks from external services and routes them to workflow triggers.

CONTEXT:
- Praxis can CALL webhooks (Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integrations/webhook.py) but cannot RECEIVE them.
- The event log at Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/event_log.py provides durable event storage with channels and cursors.
- The workflow_triggers table (migration 086) supports trigger_type='integration' with integration_id, integration_action, integration_args.
- The FastAPI app is at Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/rest.py with CORS enabled.
- Agents need to react to external events (Stripe payment, GitHub push, Slack message) to be truly autonomous.

DELIVERABLES:

1. Create Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/093_webhook_ingestion.sql:
   - Table: webhook_endpoints (
       endpoint_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT 'whep_' || substr(md5(random()::text), 1, 12),
       slug TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- e.g. 'stripe-payments', 'github-push'
       provider TEXT NOT NULL,  -- e.g. 'stripe', 'github', 'slack'
       secret_env_var TEXT,  -- env var name holding HMAC secret (e.g. 'STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET')
       signature_header TEXT,  -- header name for signature (e.g. 'Stripe-Signature', 'X-Hub-Signature-256')
       signature_algorithm TEXT DEFAULT 'hmac-sha256',  -- 'hmac-sha256', 'hmac-sha1'
       target_workflow_id TEXT,  -- workflow to trigger on valid webhook
       target_trigger_id TEXT,  -- optional: specific trigger to fire
       enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
       created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
       updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
     )
   - Table: webhook_events (
       event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT 'whev_' || substr(md5(random()::text), 1, 12),
       endpoint_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES webhook_endpoints(endpoint_id),
       payload JSONB NOT NULL,
       headers JSONB NOT NULL,
       signature_valid BOOLEAN,
       processing_status TEXT DEFAULT 'received',  -- received, processing, processed, failed
       error_message TEXT,
       received_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
       processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
     )
   - Index on webhook_events(endpoint_id, received_at DESC)
   - Index on webhook_events(processing_status) WHERE processing_status != 'processed'

2. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/integrations/webhook_receiver.py:
   - verify_signature(payload_bytes: bytes, signature: str, secret: str, algorithm: str) -> bool
     - Supports hmac-sha256 (Stripe, generic), hmac-sha1 (GitHub legacy)
     - Stripe format: split 't=...,v1=...' and verify v1
     - GitHub format: 'sha256=<hex>' prefix stripping
   - ingest_webhook(conn, endpoint_slug: str, payload: dict, headers: dict) -> WebhookIngestionResult
     - Looks up endpoint by slug
     - Verifies signature if secret_env_var is set
     - Inserts into webhook_events
     - Emits event to event_log channel 'webhook' with event_type='webhook_received'
     - If target_workflow_id set, returns trigger instruction
     - Returns: WebhookIngestionResult(event_id, signature_valid, trigger_action)
   - WebhookIngestionResult dataclass: event_id, signature_valid, trigger_action (optional dict)

3. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/webhook_ingest.py:
   - FastAPI router: webhook_ingest_router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/webhooks')
   - POST /api/webhooks/{slug} — receives webhook, calls ingest_webhook, returns 200 with event_id
   - Returns 404 if slug not found, 401 if signature invalid, 200 on success
   - Response body: {"event_id": "...", "status": "received"}
   - Must handle raw body for signature verification (use Request.body() not parsed JSON)

4. Wire the router: Add import and include_router to Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/rest.py:
   - from surfaces.api.handlers.webhook_ingest import webhook_ingest_router
   - app.include_router(webhook_ingest_router)

5. Create Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/test_webhook_receiver.py:
   - Test HMAC-SHA256 verification (valid + invalid)
   - Test Stripe signature format parsing
   - Test GitHub signature format parsing
   - Test ingestion stores event in webhook_events
   - Test unknown slug returns appropriate error
   - Test disabled endpoint rejected

PATTERNS TO FOLLOW:
- Router pattern from Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/workflow_notify.py
- Event emission pattern from Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/event_log.py (emit function)
- Connection pattern: from storage.postgres import get_workflow_pool
- Migration numbering: 093

DO NOT:
- Add authentication/API keys for the webhook endpoint itself (webhooks use signature verification)
- Implement webhook retry/replay (that's Wave 3)
- Add rate limiting to the endpoint (rate limiter integration will be wired later)

jobs.label: ipaas_w1_review
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE: Review all Wave 1 iPaaS deliverables for correctness, consistency, and adherence to Praxis patterns.

REVIEW CHECKLIST:

1. rate_limiter.py:
   - Token bucket algorithm is correct (refill rate, burst, drain on 429)
   - Retry-After parsing handles both seconds and HTTP-date
   - Thread safety for sync usage
   - No external dependencies beyond stdlib

2. http_pool.py:
   - httpx client lifecycle is correct (no leaks)
   - Cache only applies to GET/HEAD
   - LRU eviction works
   - TTL enforcement is correct

3. webhook_receiver.py + webhook_ingest.py:
   - HMAC verification is constant-time (hmac.compare_digest)
   - Stripe signature format correctly parsed
   - Raw body preserved for signature verification (not re-serialized JSON)
   - SQL injection impossible (parameterized queries)
   - Event log emission follows existing pattern

4. Migration 092 + 093:
   - Table names don't conflict with existing schema
   - Indexes are appropriate
   - Default values are sensible

5. Cross-cutting:
   - All imports reference existing modules correctly
   - No circular dependencies introduced
   - Tests are runnable with the standard pytest command
   - Code style matches existing codebase (dataclasses over Pydantic for internals, etc.)

Output a structured review with PASS/FAIL per item and any required fixes.
